concept: mkbitmap highpass filter: evening out background gradients
source_excerpt: highpass filter (evening out background gradients) ... int highpass;       /* use highpass filter? */   double lambda;      /* highpass filter radius */
implication: A highpass filter flattens slow background lightness gradients (uneven scan lighting, paper shading) so a single threshold cleanly separates foreground from background across the whole image. Without it, a gradient background gets partially traced as spurious regions/speckle, especially near the light/dark crossover.

param_mapping.rationale: No vtracer parameter exists for background flattening. INFERRED pipeline step: optionally apply a highpass/background-subtract to the source before tracing (a new pre-tracer stage alongside _presmoothed_source). Reduces spurious low-contrast regions that would otherwise inflate layer/region count. Pipeline knob, not a vtracer kwarg; only relevant when source has an uneven background.

hypothesis: Highpass-flattening the background of sources with uneven lighting before tracing will reduce spurious background regions and let filter_speckle/color_precision stay lower, improving color_fidelity and reducing junk layers; effect is null on already-flat-background art.
